detail: Slope 5 and passes through (0,-12) gives g(x)=5x-12 (choice B). Key 'c' (g(x)=5x) omits the y-intercept; rationale text says 5x-12 yet selects C. Correct answer is B.
x-intercept is where y=0: (-8,0) = choice A. Key 'b' is (0,4), which is the y-intercept. Rationale's text 'x-intercept is (-8,0)' confirms A; key letter is wrong.
Initial fee $12 grows by $0.75/year, so f(x)=12+0.75x = choice C. Key 'd' (12-0.75x) subtracts; rationale text says '12+0.75x' but selects D. Correct is C.
Table pairs give slope 50/200=0.25, so f(x)=0.25x = choice B. Key 'c' (4x) gives 4(200)=800≠50. Rationale text says 0.25x but selects C. Correct is B.
Cost = 14d + 25 = choice A. Key 'b' (25d+14) swaps rate and deposit; rationale text says '14d + 25' but selects B. Correct is A.
Tagged A2.T1 (interpret constant term) but stem asks to compute f(8)=19 — a numeric evaluation. This is A1.T1 (evaluate f(c)), not interpretation. Answer/rationale are correct.

Tagged A1.T1 (evaluate explicit f(x) at given input) but no f(x)=ax+b is supplied. Student must construct the model from words ($750 initial + $40/week × 5 weeks). This fits A3.T3 (build from rate + initial) or A1.T1 only after construction. Answer 950 is correct.
